format_notes: Pokemon Champions Regulation M-A (the game's launch regulation, active April 8 - June 17, 2026, since superseded by M-B): Mega Evolution IS legal (one Mega per battle), same as every regulation so far in this game - Serebii's Regulation M-A page describes it as allowing 'various Pokemon and Mega Evolutions.' Terastallization and Dynamax are NOT active, same as M-B. The roster is SMALLER than the current regulation - 186 species, none of the 22 species added in M-B (Metagross, Mawile, Grimmsnarl, Sceptile, Gholdengo, Blaziken, Swampert, Staraptor, etc.) were legal yet. If analyzing OLDER footage from this window, a read reporting one of those M-B-only species is almost certainly a misidentification of a similar-looking M-A-legal Pokemon (or footage that's actually from after the M-B rollover), not a real occurrence.
source_notes: Species list: Bulbapedia 'List of Pokemon in Pokemon Champions' raw wikitext (fetched 2026-07-01) - this page was captured describing exactly the 186-species M-A-era roster (it was 'stale', i.e. hadn't yet been updated with M-B's 22 additions, which is what makes it a clean M-A source). Identical to m-b.json's own m_a_base_species list - kept in sync by hand; if that list changes, mirror the change here. The 4 species in m-b.json's provisional_species were only ever observed in this project's M-B-era footage, so they are deliberately NOT included in M-A's roster below - if one of those turns out to actually predate M-B, add it here too. Regulation dates and mechanics confirmed via Serebii's Regulation M-A page and Bulbapedia's 'Regulation Sets in Pokemon Champions' page, both fetched 2026-07-02. This file won't need updating again (M-A is a closed, superseded regulation) unless a correction to the historical record itself is needed.
